reviews.review_msg: There is only so much one can say about a professor like Shawn. His teaching style was odd and clunky as he spent a lot of time going over definitions for the most basic concepts. Exam questions make little sense and often are not properly proofread before going to print. Grading is incredibly unclear and harsh with no feedback.

His accent is incredibly thick to the point that even the captions on zooms couldn't understand him. There is absolute no need to come to class and just look at the powerpoint or watch the zoom recordings. I can't state how much even if the material is easy this professor will make you wish you never took this course or was alive.

Stopped attending lectures because he spends so much time going over the answers to practice questions that he puts in his own slides. The worst I recorded was 10 minutes spent trying to figure out and explain the answers OF ONE SLIDE, he wasn't sure if what he had written made sense. Not good at explaining concepts, skip and teach yourself.

Professor Tan is clearly passionate about what he teaches, however I think his delivery can be poor and it's hard to engage with his lectures. That being said, he makes everything you need to know very accessible. Attendance is not mandatory so easy to fit into your schedule. Just be prepared to take extra time to teach yourself a bit.

Course material isnt even hard, prof just explains it incredibly slowly and poorly in lectures and is lowk hard to understand. I stopped going in person a month in and just went online, saved a ton of time. You can literally play games and not miss anything most of the time. most marks are from online quizzes, you can cheat like 60% of your grade.

his lectures are boring and his slides are concept heavy, but it isn't that hard to understand them without listening to the lecture. the grading criteria is clear and straightforward and the course content can be a bit dry sometimes. it would be harder to fail than to pass this class.

His lectures were very boring and I found it hard to follow along sometimes. If you asked a question he would not directly answer it which is bit annoying, other than that he is a fair and clear grader. as long as you watch the lectures and follow the material you cant fail this class

60 percent quizzes and 40 percent exams. His lectures are boring but straightforward. The concepts are easy to understand as long as you attend lectures which 95 percent of students don't. His lectures are blended so you can listen online or go in person. As long as you do the quizzes and practice a little you'll be fine for the course

Took this class as an elective and it was a mistake. Only good thing is he posts the lecture recordings so you don't have to go to class. Instructions aren't always clear and the tests had grammar problems which affected the responses.. made it very confusing/stressful. Weekly quizzes and papers based on that week's chapter. Sooo much writing.
He does his lectures in person and on zoom and then posts the recordings afterwards. His lectures are straightforward. You can watch the recordings on 2x speed and still understand everything.

Shawn is a great professor. He cares a lot about what he teaches. That being said I didn't show up to most lectures and still was able to do well. Everying is recorded and he does live zooms with optional attendance. You can't just read the slides wihtout watching the zooms as they are concept heavy with little text.

Professor Tan is an alright instructor. There were many topics but he demonstrated how they all connected in the realm of Cognitive Science. The grading scale is a bit different due to 6 10% MCQ Quizzes & 2 20% Integrated Tests, but the marking is fair and the questions are really easy to study for with Spaced Repetition. I'd take him again!

Shawn is a little different but once I got to know how he asks questions it was easy. I loved learning the material as it can be applied to life and studying, and it was a pretty easy class. Do the quizzes and you will pass, the 2 exams are a bonus.

meh lectures. Don't even bother showing up. lol. Quizzes are online and easy, you get 3 tries and open book. 2 Tests, worth 20% each. all MCQ. Prof also makes some typos in slides. Overall just a filler elective course if you need to take it with this prof.

Professor Tan's lectures feel useless and can sometimes be very all over the place. He also becomes extremely passive aggressive and downright not helpful when you email him or ask any questions and completely lacks the ability to adapt to things he doesn't expect.

Easy content, and weekly quizzes with 3 attempts were basically guaranteed marks. However lectures weren't very interesting, and he changed grading criteria on final project which lead to many people in the class loosing marks for no reason. Poor communication about grading and assignments

Be very careful with this professor. He is quite disorganized and frequently changes the class structure and grading abitraly. There was frequent errors made in grading that he would simply not address or be very passive aggressive about (requiring escalation to chair). Review your work extremely careful and be prepared to involve administration.

Most confusing syllabus and rubric. Grading system is strict and uses a “points” systems instead of percentages. Rubrics are vague, while using essentially a pass/fail system without any mention. Whenever students expressed confusion or distress about anything, he responded passive-aggressively or just outright provided zero clarity.

This class was pretty interesting and not too difficult content wise, but Shawn's weird decisions made things more difficult than they needed to be. He completely restructured how he distributed grades, his assignments are so poorly designed and have inconsistent criteria, which leads to inconsistent grading and overall he's a bit of a knob. Boo

Lectures were often confusing and contained mistakes. Assignments, especially the last poster one were confusing and had overtly strict and weird formatting criteria. He was also very unhelpful, and sometimes rude when contacted regarding assignment questions. Weekly quizzes were best out of 3 so that was nice at least.

Psych 201w with Shawn Tan is by far one of the easiest courses. You don't need to attend the lectures asides from the first ones just to get a general idea of the course.This course is tutorial based so attend your tutorials !! This is one of the courses that if you follow the instructions and put a bit of effort, then you will get an A.
Lectures seemed pretty pointless which was good because they were awful. Your TA is really all that matters for this course.

Professor Tan wasn't the greatest professor. I believe he was new to teaching at SFU and wasn't completely organized in his lectures. The main issue was that he had a thick accent and it made it difficult to understand what he was saying. The content was quite interesting but Shawn made it hard to be passionate about the material.

His lectures are clear and is easy to follow. He assigns quizzes every week, so get ready to read the textbook. During lectures he gives good feedback on quiz questions that most people got wrong. Attendance is not mandatory for the lecture as he hosts a zoom call that is also recorded and uploaded. Overall, nice prof.
I was worried about this course but it ended up being very straightforward and fair graded, the prof wants everyone to do well and if you complete the coursework you will do just fine

Psyc 201w with Dr.Tan was awesome, he was very accommodating to all students and was super nice. I was very afraid for this class but it was so easy and the grading criteria is painfully detailed and clear. If you follow all the instructions for the assignments, do your research participation, and do the weekly quizzes, you will get an A.

All you need to do is:
1. read slides. 
2. attend lecture.
3. memorize slides for quiz.
4. re-read all previous slides for tests (cumulative).

-If you do all the above, you should get 100% (or more).
-You're given 3 attempts for each quiz (should be free 60%).
-Bonus marks were provided for both tests, so 100% is very much achievable.

COGS110

The prof was ok, the TA was not needed, the lecture material was all common sense stuff. More memorization than learning. Did not use flash cards or mind palace.

The course was dragged out, if I had access to slides on day 1, would prob taken me max 1 week to be rdy for all the quizzes/tests.

Read>attend lecture>memorize. Easy A+
Very harsh grader with very vague instructions. He is a very nice man but as a prof he is not good. Pretty good but boring lectures, extremely nitpicky and unfair grading.
While the lecture slides may not be the most fun, this grading criteria is super clear and if you follow what he says you will get an A in the class.

I took this class online in 2020. I would recommend taking it online if you can! Cause in person you actually have to conduct the experiment. The lectures were dry but the midterms and final were easy because he gave you the terms that are on them. However, it's your TA that makes a huge difference in this PSYC class cause they grade ur paper.

He has a strong accent when explaining stuff but he recorded lectures so we could look at them to see what we missed. You only needed to be in-person for tutorials as he streams in-person lectures over zoom. I never used the "required" textbook. He struggles the most explaining concepts involving math but for the rest he explains them adequately.

He is not good at teaching. He doesn't even stick by his own grading criteria and it's really difficult to know what he is expecting. When students inevitably struggle he is not caring enough to be helpful.

Dr.Tan is a great person, but he has a strong accent which makes him hard to understand. On top of that, he is not very good at explaining concepts as he pretty much gives textbook definitions. The quizzes for this class are easy though and there was no midterm or final.

Shawn tan is a good professor. Sometimes it can be hard to understand him but his slides are clear and he is very clear about the structure of the assessments and what will be on there. The assessments come directly from the slides. He records his lectures which I love and in-person attendance isn't mandatory.

Professor Tan is a nice person, but his explanations of certain concepts are unclear and confusing. He also has difficulty answering student questions during lectures. Student participation is expected during lectures, which leaves the room quiet. But, his lecture slides are always posted, and his notes are clear.
far from clear presentations and slides, can't explain concepts clearly but expects students to be able to do that.

Horrible. He's a nice guy but not a good professor. He could not explain was he was teaching well. Class attendance was always at 50% or less. The way grading is done is not helpful and doesn't actually show if you know the material or not. Would not recommend at all.

His notes are very very easy to read. Everything is in point form and explicitly tells you what content is examinable. Some topics were not as clearly explained, but with the right questions can become much simpler. He takes feedback really well. He understands how complicated the course can be and he simplifies it really well.
